name: Gargallo - Monastery of Santa María del Olivar (PR-TE 93)
name_original: Gargallo - Monasterio de Santa María del Olivar (PR-TE 93)
region_intro: Hiking around Gargallo, located within Spain's Cultural Park of Maestrazgo, offers a range of trails through diverse landscapes. The region is characterized by its natural monuments, river sources, and historical sites such as the Monastery of Santa María del Olivar. Hikers can explore areas featuring small lakes, rock formations, and dense woodlands. The area provides 4 hiking trails, with options for moderate to difficult fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Gargallo The most popular hiking route is Ejulve - Gargallo - Las Calderas – circular route through the Cultural Park of Maestrazgo (PR-TE 93), a 15.1 miles (24.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 45 minutes to complete. This circular route traverses the varied terrain of the Cultural Park of Maestrazgo. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gargallo - Monastery of Santa María del Olivar (PR-TE 93), a difficult 18.0 miles (29.0 km) path. This route leads to the historic Monastery of Santa María del Olivar, set in a natural environment. Local hikers also love the Ejulve – Ejulve Village loop from Ejulve, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through the village surroundings, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Gargallo is defined by its cultural park landscapes, historical monasteries, and river features. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult routes. The routes in Gargallo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Gargallo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Gargallo?
A: There are 4 hiking trails available around Gargallo, offering a variety of experiences within the Cultural Park of Maestrazgo.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels of hikes in Gargallo?
A: The trails around Gargallo cater to hikers with moderate to difficult fitness levels. There are 2 moderate routes and 2 difficult routes available.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Gargallo?
A: Yes, Gargallo offers circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981496889/ejulve-gargallo-las-calderas-circular-route-through-the-cultural-park-of-maestrazgo-pr-te-93" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ejulve - Gargallo - Las Calderas – circular route through the Cultural Park of Maestrazgo (PR-TE 93)&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 15.1-mile (24.3 km) trail.

Q: What natural attractions can I expect to see while hiking in Gargallo?
A: Hikers in Gargallo can explore diverse natural landscapes. You might encounter the picturesque Balsa de Gargallo, the impressive Órganos de Montoro Natural Monument, the Cueva del Recuenco, and the Sierra de Majalinos. The region also features river sources like the Nacimiento del Río Escuriza near Ejulve.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites along the trails?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. A significant cultural site is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Santa María del Olivar&lt;/a&gt;, an Aragonese Gothic-style structure from the 16th century. You can reach it via the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981499444/gargallo-monastery-of-santa-maria-del-olivar-pr-te-93" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gargallo - Monastery of Santa María del Olivar (PR-TE 93)&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to visit near Gargallo?
A: Yes, near the town of Molinos, you can find the spectacular Salto de Agua de San Nicolás, a 30-meter high waterfall adjacent to a hermitage of the same name.

Q: Is Gargallo suitable for family hiking?
A: While some routes are challenging, there are moderate options that families with good fitness levels might enjoy. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31441384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ejulve – Ejulve Village loop from Ejulve&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.4-mile (11.9 km) trail.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Gargallo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, cultural park landscapes, and historical sites like the Monastery of Santa María del Olivar.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Gargallo?
A: The region's diverse landscapes can be enjoyed across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking, with autumn potentially showcasing beautiful foliage.

Q: Are there options for parking near the hiking trails in Gargallo?
A: Many hiking trails in the Cultural Park of Maestrazgo, including those around Gargallo, typically have designated parking areas near their starting points in towns like Gargallo or Ejulve. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information.

Q: What towns can I explore near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond Gargallo itself, you can explore nearby towns such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5290366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ejulve&lt;/a&gt;, which is a starting point for 'The Silent Route' and features the Nacimiento del Río Escuriza. Molinos is another town known for its waterfall.
tips:
The Sanctuary/Monastery of Santa María del Olivar is located in Estercuel, within the natural setting of the Serranía de Montalbán mountain range, in the Andorra-Sierra de Arcos region.

Although often referred to as a sanctuary, its official name is the Monastery of Santa María del Olivar. It is one of the most important historical and artistic monuments in the area and has been a significant spiritual center for over seven centuries.
Located 4 km from Estercuel, in the province of Teruel, it lies within the river valley and is surrounded by pine forests.
It sits atop a hill at an altitude of 700 meters, overlooking the valley watered by the Escuriza River.

We are in the town of Gargallo, located at the foot of Muela Alta. Another of the beautiful towns in Teruel where we highlight the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Piedad, the Hermitage of San Blas or the Nacimiento del Río Escuriza.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Santa María del Olivar
